rawText:
So, artificial intelligence is known for disrupting all kinds of industries. What about ice cream? What kind of mind-blowing new flavors could we generate with the power of an advanced artificial intelligence? So I teamed up with a group of coders from Kealing Middle School to find out the answer to this question. They collected over 1,600 existing ice cream flavors, and together, we fed them to an algorithm to see what it would generate. And here are some of the flavors that the AI came up with.

These flavors are not delicious, as we might have hoped they would be. So the question is: What happened? What went wrong? Is the AI trying to kill us? Or is it trying to do what we asked, and there was a problem?

In movies, when something goes wrong with AI, it's usually because the AI has decided that it doesn't want to obey the humans anymore, and it's got its own goals, thank you very much. In real life, though, the AI that we actually have is not nearly smart enough for that. It has the approximate computing power of an earthworm, or maybe at most a single honeybee, and actually, probably maybe less. Like, we're constantly learning new things about brains that make it clear how much our AIs don't measure up to real brains.

So today's AI can do a task like identify a pedestrian in a picture, but it doesn't have a concept of what the pedestrian is beyond that it's a collection of lines and textures and things. It doesn't know what a human actually is. So will today's AI do what we ask it to do? It will if it can, but it might not do what we actually want.

So let's say that you were trying to get an AI to take this collection of robot parts and assemble them into some kind of robot to get from Point A to Point B. Now, if you were going to try and solve this problem by writing a traditional-style computer program, you would give the program step-by-step instructions on how to take these parts, how to assemble them into a robot with legs and then how to use those legs to walk to Point B.

But when you're using AI to solve the problem, it goes differently. You don't tell it how to solve the problem, you just give it the goal, and it has to figure out for itself via trial and error how to reach that goal. And it turns out that the way AI tends to solve this particular problem is by doing this: it assembles itself into a tower and then falls over and lands at Point B. And technically, this solves the problem. Technically, it got to Point B.

The danger of AI is not that it's going to rebel against us, it's that it's going to do exactly what we ask it to do. So then the trick of working with AI becomes: How do we set up the problem so that it actually does what we want?

overview.title: The Danger of AI Is Weirder Than You Think

sentences.original: So, artificial intelligence is known for disrupting all kinds of industries.
sentences.translation: 인공지능은 모든 종류의 산업을 뒤흔드는 것으로 알려져 있습니다.

vocabulary.example: Artificial intelligence is transforming healthcare.
Smartphones disrupted the camera industry.
The tech industry is growing rapidly.

expressions.usage: 어떤 대상의 대표적 특징이나 명성을 소개할 때
다양성을 강조하며 폭넓은 범위를 나타낼 때

patterns.pattern: [Subject] is known for [verb-ing] ...
patterns.explanation: "~로 알려져 있다"는 수동태 패턴. for 뒤에 동명사가 와서 이유/특징을 설명.
examples: Korea is known for producing high-quality electronics.
She is known for speaking her mind.

drillQuestions.question: Artificial intelligence is known _____ disrupting industries.
AI is known for _____ all kinds of industries.

sentences.notes: "be known for + 동명사"는 수동태 + 전치사 + 동명사 구조. disrupt는 실리콘밸리에서 유래한 비즈니스 용어로, 기존 산업을 근본적으로 변화시키는 혁신을 뜻한다.

koreanAlignment.en: artificial intelligence
is known for
disrupting all kinds of industries

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 도입(hook) — 익숙한 AI 화두를 꺼내며 청중의 관심을 끄는 시작

sentences.original: What kind of mind-blowing new flavors could we generate with the power of an advanced artificial intelligence?
sentences.translation: 첨단 인공지능의 힘으로 어떤 종류의 기상천외한 새로운 맛을 만들어 낼 수 있을까요?

grammarElements.text: with the power of an advanced artificial intelligence
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (수단)

vocabulary.example: The special effects were mind-blowing.
This ice cream comes in many flavors.
The software can generate reports automatically.

expressions.usage: 종류를 물을 때 사용하는 의문 표현
어떤 도구나 능력을 수단으로 제시할 때

patterns.pattern: What kind of [noun] could we [verb] with [tool/method]?
patterns.explanation: 가능성을 탐구하는 의문문 패턴. could는 가능성/추측을 나타내는 조동사.
examples: What kind of solutions could we find with this approach?
What kind of music could we create with AI?

drillQuestions.question: What _____ of new flavors could we generate?
What kind of flavors could we generate _____ the power of AI?

sentences.notes: mind-blowing은 하이픈으로 연결된 복합형용사. 의문문에서 could + 주어 도치(could we generate)가 사용되어 탐구적 톤을 만든다. 아이스크림이라는 가벼운 소재로 AI라는 무거운 주제에 접근하는 유머러스한 전략.

koreanAlignment.en: What kind of mind-blowing new flavors
could we generate
with the power of an advanced artificial intelligence

sentences.original: So I teamed up with a group of coders from Kealing Middle School to find out the answer to this question.
sentences.translation: 그래서 저는 킬링 중학교의 코더 그룹과 팀을 이뤄 이 질문에 대한 답을 알아보았습니다.

grammarElements.text: a group of coders from Kealing Middle School
grammarElements.label: 전치사 목적어

grammarElements.text: to find out the answer to this question
grammarElements.label: 목적 부사구 (to부정사)

vocabulary.example: We teamed up with a local company for the project.
The coders worked on the new application.
I need to find out what happened.

expressions.usage: 협력 관계를 시작할 때
질문이나 문제에 대한 해답을 추구할 때

patterns.pattern: I teamed up with [person/group] to [verb] ...
patterns.explanation: 협력 대상과 목적을 함께 소개하는 패턴. TED 강연에서 프로젝트 배경을 설명할 때 자주 사용.
examples: I teamed up with researchers to study the effect.
We teamed up with engineers to build a prototype.

drillQuestions.question: I teamed _____ with a group of coders.
We worked together to find _____ the answer.

sentences.notes: team up with는 비격식 구동사로 TED 강연의 친근한 톤에 잘 맞는다. 중학생(Kealing Middle School)과 협력했다는 점이 AI를 친근하게 만드는 전략의 일부.

koreanAlignment.en: So I teamed up with
a group of coders from Kealing Middle School
to find out the answer to this question
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 질문에 대한 답을 알아보기 위해

sentences.original: They collected over 1,600 existing ice cream flavors, and together, we fed them to an algorithm to see what it would generate.
sentences.translation: 그들은 기존의 아이스크림 맛 1,600개 이상을 수집했고, 함께 이것들을 알고리즘에 넣어 무엇이 생성되는지 확인했습니다.

grammarElements.text: and together, we fed them to an algorithm
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (and)

grammarElements.text: to see what it would generate
grammarElements.label: 목적 부사구 (to부정사 + 간접의문문)

vocabulary.example: Scientists collected data from thousands of participants.
The algorithm processes millions of data points.
We fed the data into the computer.

expressions.usage: feed의 과거형. 데이터를 시스템에 입력하는 것을 비유적으로 "먹이다"로 표현

expressions.phrase: to see what it would generate
expressions.usage: 실험의 목적을 소개할 때. what + would로 미래 결과에 대한 호기심 표현

patterns.pattern: We fed [data] to [system] to see what it would [verb]
patterns.explanation: 데이터를 시스템에 입력하고 결과를 기대하는 실험 서술 패턴.
examples: We fed the images to a neural network to see what it would classify.
We fed customer reviews to the model to see what it would predict.

drillQuestions.question: We _____ the data to the algorithm.
We wanted to see what it _____ generate.

sentences.notes: feed-fed-fed는 불규칙 동사. "feed data to an algorithm"은 기술 분야에서 자주 쓰이는 표현으로, 마치 기계에 먹이를 주듯 데이터를 입력한다는 비유적 표현이다.

koreanAlignment.en: They collected over 1,600 existing ice cream flavors
and together, we fed them to an algorithm
to see what it would generate

sentences.original: These flavors are not delicious, as we might have hoped they would be.
sentences.translation: 이 맛들은 우리가 기대했을 만큼 맛있지 않습니다.

grammarElements.text: as we might have hoped they would be
grammarElements.label: 종속절 (비교 — as)

vocabulary.example: The cake was absolutely delicious.

expressions.usage: 기대와 현실의 괴리를 절제된 방식으로 표현. 영국식 언더스테이트먼트(understatement) 느낌.
과거에 가졌을 수 있는 기대를 가정법으로 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] is not [adjective], as we might have hoped [it/they] would be
patterns.explanation: 기대와 현실 사이의 실망을 절제된 톤으로 표현하는 패턴. might have + 과거분사로 비현실적 가정.
examples: The results are not promising, as we might have hoped they would be.
The movie was not great, as we might have hoped it would be.

drillQuestions.question: The flavors are not delicious, _____ we might have hoped.
We might _____ hoped they would be delicious.

sentences.notes: "might have hoped"는 가정법 과거완료로, 실제로는 이루어지지 않은 기대를 나타낸다. 유머러스한 언더스테이트먼트(절제 표현)로, "맛없다"를 "기대만큼 맛있지 않다"로 에둘러 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: These flavors are not delicious
as we might have hoped they would be

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 절제 표현(understatement) — "맛없다"를 우회적으로 표현하여 유머 효과

sentences.original: So the question is: What happened? What went wrong? Is the AI trying to kill us? Or is it trying to do what we asked, and there was a problem?
sentences.translation: 그래서 질문은 이것입니다: 무슨 일이 일어난 걸까요? 무엇이 잘못된 걸까요? AI가 우리를 죽이려고 하는 걸까요? 아니면 우리가 시킨 것을 하려고 했는데, 문제가 있었던 걸까요?

grammarElements.text: Is the AI trying to kill us?
grammarElements.label: 의문문 (현재진행 — Yes/No)

grammarElements.text: Or is it trying to do what we asked, and there was a problem?
grammarElements.label: 의문문 (대안 제시 — Or)

vocabulary.example: Everything that could go wrong did go wrong.

expressions.usage: 핵심 문제를 제기하며 청중의 주의를 집중시킬 때
실패나 문제의 원인을 탐구할 때

patterns.pattern: Is [Subject] trying to [verb A]? Or is it trying to [verb B]?
patterns.explanation: 두 가지 가능한 해석을 제시하며 청중에게 생각할 거리를 던지는 수사적 의문문 패턴.
examples: Is the system trying to help us? Or is it trying to maximize profit?
Is she trying to be kind? Or is she trying to avoid conflict?

drillQuestions.question: What went _____?
Is the AI trying _____ kill us?

sentences.notes: 연속 의문문 4개를 사용하는 수사적 기법. "Is the AI trying to kill us?"는 청중의 웃음을 유도하는 과장법이며, 마지막 질문("Or is it trying to do what we asked")이 강연의 진짜 논점을 암시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: the question is
What happened?
What went wrong?
Is the AI trying to kill us?
Or is it trying to do what we asked
koreanAlignment.ko: 아니면 우리가 시킨 것을 하려고 했는데

koreanAlignment.en: and there was a problem?

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 수사적 의문문(rhetorical questions) — 연속된 질문으로 긴장감을 만들고 강연의 핵심 논점을 설정

sentences.original: In movies, when something goes wrong with AI, it's usually because the AI has decided that it doesn't want to obey the humans anymore, and it's got its own goals, thank you very much.
sentences.translation: 영화에서는 AI에 문제가 생기면, 보통 AI가 더 이상 인간에게 복종하고 싶지 않다고 결정하고 자기만의 목표를 가지게 되었기 때문입니다.

grammarElements.text: when something goes wrong with AI
grammarElements.label: 종속절 (시간/조건 — when)

grammarElements.text: the AI has decided that it doesn't want to obey the humans anymore
grammarElements.label: that절 (이유)

grammarElements.text: and it's got its own goals
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (and)

vocabulary.example: Soldiers must obey orders.
I don't want to wait anymore.

expressions.usage: 어떤 시스템이나 상황에 오류가 발생할 때
일반적인 원인을 설명할 때
여기서는 AI의 오만한 태도를 풍자적으로 흉내 내는 삽입구

patterns.pattern: When [something happens], it's usually because [reason]
patterns.explanation: 일반적 원인-결과를 설명하는 패턴. 현재 시제로 일반적 진실/패턴을 표현.
examples: When students fail, it's usually because they didn't prepare.
When apps crash, it's usually because of a memory leak.

drillQuestions.question: When something goes wrong with AI, it's usually _____ the AI has decided to rebel.
The AI doesn't want to _____ the humans anymore.

sentences.notes: "thank you very much"는 문장 끝에 풍자적으로 덧붙인 표현. AI가 마치 "제 갈 길 가겠습니다, 감사합니다"라고 말하는 것처럼 의인화한 유머. 영어 구어에서 이런 풍자적 "thank you very much"는 불만이나 냉소를 나타낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: In movies
when something goes wrong with AI
it's usually because
the AI has decided that it doesn't want to obey the humans anymore
koreanAlignment.ko: AI가 더 이상 인간에게 복종하고 싶지 않다고 결정하고

koreanAlignment.en: and it's got its own goals
thank you very much

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 풍자(irony) — "thank you very much"로 영화 속 AI의 오만함을 유머러스하게 흉내

sentences.original: In real life, though, the AI that we actually have is not nearly smart enough for that.
sentences.translation: 하지만 현실에서는 우리가 실제로 가지고 있는 AI는 그렇게 할 만큼 충분히 똑똑하지 않습니다.

grammarElements.text: the AI that we actually have
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계대명사절 포함)

vocabulary.example: That's not nearly enough money.

expressions.usage: 가상이나 이론과 대비하여 실제 상황을 언급할 때
기대치에 크게 못 미침을 강조

patterns.pattern: In [context A], [X happens]. In [context B], though, [Y happens].
patterns.explanation: 두 상황(영화 vs 현실)을 대비하는 패턴. though를 삽입하여 전환 효과를 줌.
examples: In theory, it should work. In practice, though, it fails.
In the lab, it was perfect. In the field, though, problems arose.

drillQuestions.question: In real life, _____, AI is not that smart.
AI is not _____ smart enough for that.

sentences.notes: "not nearly"는 "not at all"보다 부드럽지만 "부족함"을 강하게 전달하는 표현. though가 문장 중간에 삽입되어 앞 문장과의 대조를 자연스럽게 만든다.

koreanAlignment.en: In real life, though
the AI that we actually have
is not nearly smart enough
for that

sentences.original: It has the approximate computing power of an earthworm, or maybe at most a single honeybee, and actually, probably maybe less.
sentences.translation: 지렁이 정도의 대략적인 연산 능력을 가지고 있거나, 기껏해야 꿀벌 한 마리 정도이고, 사실 아마 그보다 더 적을 수도 있습니다.

grammarElements.text: the approximate computing power of an earthworm
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: or maybe at most a single honeybee
grammarElements.label: 대안 제시 (or)

vocabulary.example: The approximate cost is $500.
Modern smartphones have incredible computing power.
Earthworms are important for soil health.
Honeybees are essential for pollination.

expressions.usage: 상한선을 표현할 때. 기대치가 높지 않음을 암시
확신 없이 점점 하향 조정하는 구어적 표현. 유머 효과.

patterns.pattern: [Subject] has the [adjective] [noun] of [A], or maybe [B], and actually, probably [even less/more]
patterns.explanation: 비교 대상을 점진적으로 조정하며 유머를 만드는 패턴. 기대를 계속 낮추는 효과.
examples: It has the speed of a turtle, or maybe a snail, and actually, probably less.

drillQuestions.question: AI has the _____ computing power of an earthworm.
Or maybe at _____ a single honeybee.

sentences.notes: "probably maybe less"에서 probably와 maybe를 겹쳐 쓰는 것은 문법적으로 불필요하지만, 의도적으로 중첩하여 "확신할 수 없다"는 느낌을 강조하는 구어체 유머. 지렁이와 꿀벌 비유로 AI의 한계를 극적으로 축소하여 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: It has the approximate computing power
of an earthworm
or maybe at most a single honeybee
and actually, probably maybe less
koreanAlignment.ko: 사실 아마 그보다 더 적을 수도

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 점강법(anticlimax) — 지렁이 → 꿀벌 → 그보다 더 적다로 점점 낮추며 웃음 유발

sentences.original: Like, we're constantly learning new things about brains that make it clear how much our AIs don't measure up to real brains.
sentences.translation: 우리는 끊임없이 뇌에 대한 새로운 것들을 배우고 있는데, 이는 우리의 AI가 실제 뇌에 얼마나 미치지 못하는지를 분명하게 해줍니다.

grammarElements.text: that make it clear how much our AIs don't measure up to real brains
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (that — things를 수식)

vocabulary.example: Technology is constantly evolving.
His performance didn't measure up to expectations.

expressions.usage: 어떤 사실을 명확히 드러낼 때
기준이나 비교 대상에 도달하지 못할 때

patterns.pattern: We're constantly learning [things] that make it clear [how/what/why] ...
patterns.explanation: 지속적 발견이 어떤 사실을 분명히 한다는 구조. that절 안에 간접의문문이 내포.
examples: We're constantly learning facts that make it clear how much we have to improve.
We're constantly discovering things that make it clear why the old approach failed.

drillQuestions.question: We're constantly _____ new things about brains.
Our AIs don't measure _____ to real brains.

sentences.notes: "Like"로 문장을 시작하는 것은 매우 구어적인 담화표지. TED 강연에서 자연스러운 대화체를 유지하기 위해 의도적으로 사용. "measure up to"는 기대나 기준에 도달하는지를 비유적으로 표현하는 구동사.

koreanAlignment.en: we're constantly learning new things about brains
koreanAlignment.ko: 뇌에 대해 끊임없이 새로운 것을 배우고 있습니다

koreanAlignment.en: that make it clear
how much our AIs don't measure up to
real brains

sentences.original: So today's AI can do a task like identify a pedestrian in a picture, but it doesn't have a concept of what the pedestrian is beyond that it's a collection of lines and textures and things.
sentences.translation: 오늘날의 AI는 사진에서 보행자를 식별하는 것 같은 작업을 할 수 있지만, 보행자가 선과 텍스처와 사물의 집합체라는 것 이상으로 보행자가 무엇인지에 대한 개념은 없습니다.

grammarElements.text: a task like identify a pedestrian in a picture
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (like + 동사원형)

grammarElements.text: but it doesn't have a concept of
grammarElements.label: 대조절 (but)

grammarElements.text: beyond that it's a collection of lines and textures and things
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (범위 제한 — beyond)

vocabulary.example: Drivers must yield to pedestrians at crosswalks.
The concept of time is hard to define.
The algorithm analyzes textures in images.
He has a large collection of stamps.

expressions.usage: 이해력의 한계를 지적할 때
범위나 한계를 설정할 때

patterns.pattern: [Subject] can do [task], but it doesn't have a concept of [deeper understanding]
patterns.explanation: 표면적 능력과 진정한 이해의 차이를 대조하는 패턴. AI의 한계를 설명할 때 핵심적.
examples: A calculator can do math, but it doesn't have a concept of what numbers mean.
Translation AI can convert text, but it doesn't have a concept of cultural context.

drillQuestions.question: AI can do a task _____ identify a pedestrian.
It doesn't have a _____ of what the pedestrian is.

sentences.notes: "a task like identify"에서 like 뒤에 동사원형이 오는 것은 구어체 특유의 용법. 격식체에서는 "such as identifying"이 정확하다. "beyond that"은 AI의 이해 범위가 표면적 패턴 인식에 한정됨을 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: today's AI can do a task
koreanAlignment.ko: 오늘날의 AI는 작업을 할 수 있습니다

koreanAlignment.en: like identify a pedestrian in a picture
but it doesn't have a concept of
what the pedestrian is
beyond that it's a collection of lines and textures

sentences.original: It doesn't know what a human actually is.
sentences.translation: 인간이 실제로 무엇인지 모릅니다.

vocabulary.example: What does happiness actually mean?

expressions.phrase: doesn't know what X actually is
expressions.usage: 본질적 이해의 부재를 표현. AI의 근본적 한계를 짚는 표현.

patterns.pattern: It doesn't know what [noun] actually is.
patterns.explanation: 어떤 개념의 본질에 대한 이해가 없음을 간결하게 표현하는 패턴.
examples: It doesn't know what kindness actually is.
The system doesn't know what a safe driver actually is.

drillQuestions.question: It doesn't know _____ a human actually is.

sentences.notes: 의도적으로 짧고 단순한 문장. 앞의 복잡한 설명을 한 줄로 요약하여 충격적인 결론을 전달한다. "actually"가 "진짜로, 본질적으로"라는 의미를 강조.

koreanAlignment.en: It doesn't know
what a human actually is

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 간결한 요약(punchline) — 복잡한 기술 설명을 한 문장으로 압축하여 핵심 메시지 전달

sentences.original: So will today's AI do what we ask it to do? It will if it can, but it might not do what we actually want.
sentences.translation: 그렇다면 오늘날의 AI는 우리가 시키는 것을 할까요? 할 수 있다면 하겠지만, 우리가 실제로 원하는 것은 하지 않을 수 있습니다.

grammarElements.text: what we ask it to do
grammarElements.label: 간접의문문 (목적어절)

vocabulary.example: I asked him to help me.

expressions.usage: 조건부 긍정을 간결하게 표현. if it can으로 능력의 제한을 암시.
"우리가 시킨 것"과 "실제로 원하는 것" 사이의 괴리를 강조

patterns.pattern: Will [Subject] do what we ask? It will if it can, but it might not do what we actually want.
patterns.explanation: "시킨 것"과 "원하는 것"의 차이를 대조하는 강연의 핵심 패턴.
examples: Will the robot follow instructions? It will if it can, but it might not achieve what we actually intended.

drillQuestions.question: It will _____ it can.
It might not do what we _____ want.

sentences.notes: 이 문장이 강연의 핵심 논제(thesis)다. "what we ask it to do"(우리가 시킨 것)와 "what we actually want"(실제 원하는 것) 사이의 차이가 AI 문제의 본질이라는 주장. "It will if it can"은 축약된 조건문으로 구어체의 간결함을 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: will today's AI do
what we ask it to do
It will if it can
but it might not do
what we actually want

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 핵심 논제 제시(thesis statement) — 강연 전체의 핵심 주장을 질문-답변 형식으로 제시

sentences.original: So let's say that you were trying to get an AI to take this collection of robot parts and assemble them into some kind of robot to get from Point A to Point B.
sentences.translation: 이 로봇 부품들을 가지고 어떤 로봇으로 조립해서 A 지점에서 B 지점으로 가게 하려고 AI에게 시켰다고 합시다.

grammarElements.text: you were trying to get an AI to
grammarElements.label: 가정절 (과거진행)

grammarElements.text: take this collection of robot parts
grammarElements.label: 목적보어 1 (take)

grammarElements.text: and assemble them into some kind of robot
grammarElements.label: 목적보어 2 (assemble)

grammarElements.text: to get from Point A to Point B
grammarElements.label: 목적 부사구 (to부정사)

vocabulary.example: The workers assembled the furniture.
There is a collection of tools on the table.

expressions.usage: 가상의 시나리오를 소개할 때. TED 강연에서 청중을 시나리오에 끌어들이는 장치.
사역 구조. get + 목적어 + to부정사로 동작을 지시

expressions.phrase: from Point A to Point B
expressions.usage: 이동의 출발점과 도착점을 추상적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: Let's say that you were trying to get [X] to [verb] ...
patterns.explanation: 가상 시나리오를 설정하는 패턴. 과거진행(were trying)으로 가정의 느낌을 강화.
examples: Let's say that you were trying to get a robot to clean your house.
Let's say that you were trying to get an AI to write a song.

drillQuestions.question: Let's _____ that you were trying to build a robot.
You were trying to get an AI _____ assemble the parts.

sentences.notes: "get + 목적어 + to부정사"는 사역동사 구조. make와 달리 get은 to가 필요하다. "let's say"는 구어에서 가설을 도입하는 매우 자연스러운 표현으로, "suppose" 또는 "imagine"의 비격식 버전.

koreanAlignment.en: let's say that
you were trying to get an AI to
take this collection of robot parts
and assemble them into some kind of robot
to get from Point A to Point B

sentences.original: Now, if you were going to try and solve this problem by writing a traditional-style computer program, you would give the program step-by-step instructions on how to take these parts, how to assemble them into a robot with legs and then how to use those legs to walk to Point B.
sentences.translation: 만약 전통적인 방식의 컴퓨터 프로그램을 작성해서 이 문제를 풀려고 했다면, 이 부품들을 어떻게 가져다가, 다리가 있는 로봇으로 조립하고, 그 다리로 B 지점까지 걸어가는 방법에 대한 단계별 지시를 프로그램에 줘야 할 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: if you were going to try and solve this problem
grammarElements.label: 조건절 (가정법 과거)

grammarElements.text: by writing a traditional-style computer program
grammarElements.label: 수단 전치사구 (by + 동명사)

grammarElements.text: on how to take these parts, how to assemble them ... and then how to use those legs to walk
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (지시 내용 — how to 3개 병렬)

vocabulary.example: We used a traditional-style approach.
Follow the step-by-step instructions.
Please read the instructions carefully.

expressions.usage: "try to solve"의 구어적 변형. 비격식 영어에서 "try and + 동사원형"이 흔히 사용됨.
절차적 방법을 설명할 때

patterns.pattern: If you were going to [verb] by [method], you would [verb] ...
patterns.explanation: 가정법 과거 패턴. 비현실적이거나 대안적 시나리오를 설명.
examples: If you were going to learn by reading, you would need many books.
If you were going to fix this by coding, you would write a script.

drillQuestions.question: If you were going to solve this problem _____ writing a program, you would give instructions.
You would give the program step-_____-step instructions.

sentences.notes: "try and solve"는 구어에서 "try to solve" 대신 자주 쓰인다. "how to" 3개가 병렬로 연결되어(how to take, how to assemble, how to use) 전통적 프로그래밍의 단계적 성격을 반영한다.

koreanAlignment.en: if you were going to try and solve this problem
by writing a traditional-style computer program
you would give the program step-by-step instructions
koreanAlignment.ko: 프로그램에 단계별 지시를 줘야 할 것입니다

koreanAlignment.en: on how to take these parts
how to assemble them into a robot with legs
and then how to use those legs to walk to Point B
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 다리로 B 지점까지 걸어가는 방법

sentences.original: But when you're using AI to solve the problem, it goes differently.
sentences.translation: 하지만 AI를 사용해서 문제를 풀 때는 다르게 진행됩니다.

grammarElements.text: when you're using AI to solve the problem
grammarElements.label: 종속절 (시간/조건 — when)

vocabulary.example: Each person handles stress differently.

expressions.usage: 과정이나 방식이 예상과 다를 때

patterns.pattern: But when you're [verb-ing] [X] to [verb], it goes differently.
patterns.explanation: 전환 문장으로, 이전 방법과 새로운 방법의 차이를 도입하는 패턴.
examples: But when you're using data to make decisions, it goes differently.
But when you're relying on intuition to navigate, it goes differently.

drillQuestions.question: When you're using AI to solve the problem, it goes _____.

sentences.notes: 간결한 전환 문장으로, 전통적 프로그래밍(앞 문단)에서 AI 방식(이 문단)으로의 전환을 알린다. "it goes differently"는 매우 구어적이고 직관적인 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: But when you're using AI to solve the problem
koreanAlignment.ko: 하지만 AI를 사용해서 문제를 풀 때는

koreanAlignment.en: it goes differently

sentences.original: You don't tell it how to solve the problem, you just give it the goal, and it has to figure out for itself via trial and error how to reach that goal.
sentences.translation: 문제를 어떻게 풀지 알려주지 않고, 그냥 목표만 주면, AI가 시행착오를 통해 스스로 그 목표에 도달하는 방법을 알아내야 합니다.

grammarElements.text: don't tell it how to solve the problem
grammarElements.label: 주절 (부정)

grammarElements.text: you just give it the goal
grammarElements.label: 등위절

grammarElements.text: and it has to figure out for itself
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (의무 — has to)

vocabulary.example: I need to figure out the solution.
We learned through trial and error.
We communicated via email.

expressions.usage: 외부 도움 없이 독립적으로 해결할 때
반복적인 실험과 실패를 거쳐 해결하는 과정

patterns.pattern: You don't tell it [how to X], you just give it [the goal], and it has to figure out [how to Y]
patterns.explanation: AI/머신러닝의 핵심 원리를 설명하는 패턴. 지시 부재 → 목표 부여 → 자율 탐색의 3단계.
examples: You don't tell it which path to take, you just give it the destination, and it has to figure out the route.

drillQuestions.question: You just give it the goal, and it has to figure _____ how to reach it.
AI learns via trial and _____.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 전통적 프로그래밍과 AI/머신러닝의 핵심적 차이를 설명한다. "trial and error"는 과학에서 온 용어로, AI의 학습 방식(강화학습 등)을 직관적으로 비유한다.

koreanAlignment.en: You don't tell it how to solve the problem
you just give it the goal
and it has to figure out for itself
via trial and error
how to reach that goal

sentences.original: And it turns out that the way AI tends to solve this particular problem is by doing this: it assembles itself into a tower and then falls over and lands at Point B.
sentences.translation: 그리고 AI가 이 특정 문제를 풀려고 하는 방식은 이것입니다: 스스로를 탑으로 조립한 다음 넘어져서 B 지점에 착지합니다.

grammarElements.text: the way AI tends to solve this particular problem
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계부사 생략 — the way)

grammarElements.text: it assembles itself into a tower
grammarElements.label: 설명절 1

grammarElements.text: and then falls over and lands at Point B
grammarElements.label: 설명절 2, 3 (연속 동작)

vocabulary.example: It turns out that the experiment was wrong.
People tend to eat more in winter.
The child fell over while running.
The plane landed safely.

expressions.usage: 예상과 다른 결과를 소개할 때. 놀라움의 뉘앙스.
반복적 패턴이나 경향을 설명할 때

patterns.pattern: It turns out that the way [Subject] tends to [verb] is by [doing something unexpected]
patterns.explanation: 예상 밖의 결과를 드러내는 서사 패턴. 기대 → 반전의 구조.
examples: It turns out that the way students tend to study is by cramming the night before.
It turns out that the way cats solve problems is by ignoring them.

drillQuestions.question: It turns _____ that AI solves problems differently.
AI _____ to solve problems in unexpected ways.

sentences.notes: 이 문장이 강연의 가장 큰 웃음 포인트 중 하나. "탑으로 조립 → 넘어짐 → B 지점 도착"이라는 AI의 기발한(?) 해결책은 AI가 규칙의 허점을 찾는 경향을 보여주는 대표적 사례다.

koreanAlignment.en: it turns out that
the way AI tends to solve this particular problem
koreanAlignment.ko: AI가 이 특정 문제를 풀려는 방식은

koreanAlignment.en: is by doing this
it assembles itself into a tower
and then falls over
and lands at Point B

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 반전(surprise/punchline) — 예상 밖의 AI 행동을 폭로하며 폭소 유도

sentences.original: And technically, this solves the problem. Technically, it got to Point B.
sentences.translation: 그리고 기술적으로 이것은 문제를 해결합니다. 기술적으로 B 지점에 도착했습니다.

grammarElements.text: Technically, it got to Point B
grammarElements.label: 반복 강조절

vocabulary.example: Technically, he broke the rule, but no one noticed.
Can you solve this equation?

expressions.usage: "틀린 것은 아니지만 기대와 다르다"는 뉘앙스. 풍자적으로 사용.

patterns.pattern: Technically, [this/it] [verb] [the goal]. Technically, [it] [achieved the result].
patterns.explanation: "기술적으로는 맞다"는 양보적 인정을 반복하여 풍자 효과를 만드는 패턴.
examples: Technically, the student passed the test. Technically, 60% is a passing grade.
Technically, the app works. Technically, it displays the page.

drillQuestions.question: _____, this solves the problem.

sentences.notes: "technically"를 두 번 반복하여 풍자 효과를 극대화. "기술적으로는 맞지만 우리가 원한 건 이게 아니다"라는 함의. 이 단어가 강연 전체에서 반복적으로 사용되는 키워드이다.

koreanAlignment.en: And technically, this solves the problem
Technically, it got to Point B

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 반복(repetition) + 풍자(irony) — "technically"를 반복하여 AI의 "기술적으로 정확하지만 본질적으로 틀린" 행동을 풍자

sentences.original: The danger of AI is not that it's going to rebel against us, it's that it's going to do exactly what we ask it to do.
sentences.translation: AI의 위험은 우리에게 반란을 일으키는 것이 아니라, 우리가 시킨 것을 정확히 하는 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: it's going to rebel against us
grammarElements.label: that절 1 (부정 대상)

grammarElements.text: it's going to do exactly what we ask it to do
grammarElements.label: that절 2 (진짜 위험)

vocabulary.example: There is a danger of flooding.
Teenagers often rebel against their parents.
Do exactly as I say.

expressions.phrase: the danger of X is not Y, it's Z
expressions.usage: 일반적 오해를 바로잡고 진짜 위험을 지적할 때
권위나 통제에 저항할 때

expressions.phrase: exactly what we ask it to do
expressions.usage: "정확히"를 강조하여 의도와 결과의 아이러니를 부각

patterns.pattern: The danger of [X] is not that [common fear], it's that [real danger]
patterns.explanation: 일반적 두려움을 부정하고 진짜 위험을 재정의하는 대비 패턴. 강연의 제목과 직결되는 핵심 주장.
examples: The danger of social media is not that it's addictive, it's that it distorts our view of reality.
The danger of automation is not that robots will take over, it's that we won't adapt fast enough.

drillQuestions.question: The danger of AI is not that it's going to _____ against us.
It's going to do _____ what we ask it to do.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 강연 전체의 핵심 메시지이자 제목의 직접적 설명. "not X, it's Y" 대비 구조가 청중의 기존 인식(터미네이터식 AI 공포)을 뒤엎고 새로운 관점(AI가 너무 순종적이라는 위험)을 제시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The danger of AI
is not that it's going to rebel against us
it's that it's going to do exactly
what we ask it to do

pronunciationNotes: rebel: /rɪˈbel/ — "리벨" (동사일 때 강세는 두 번째 음절)
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 재정의(reframing) — AI 위험에 대한 대중적 오해를 뒤집고 핵심 논점을 한 문장으로 재정의
